thesis_summary: A sector-agnostic pre-seed and seed-stage fund that invests in Indian 'moonshot' ideas using a simplified iSAFE note model. They focus on providing the first institutional capital and high-touch mentorship to help startups achieve hyper-growth and secure follow-on funding.
key_partners: Sanjay Mehta (Founder & Partner, known for his extensive angel investment career and helping founders win)
Ninad Karpe (Founder & Partner, former CEO of public listed companies known for scaling business growth)
Vatsal Kanakiya (CEO, known for futurism and mapping technology trends)
what_they_talk_about: Hyper-growth metrics, India SAFE (iSAFE) notes to simplify legal hurdles, 'Moonshot' philosophy, cohort-based investing, and helping founders reach their next funding round.
what_they_prioritize_in_founders: Explicitly look for 'hustle' and a growth mindset. They prioritize founders who demonstrate the ambition to build massive scale quickly rather than stable, slow-growing businesses.
typical_questions_they_ask: Can this model grow 50% to 100% month-on-month in the beginning?
Is this a moonshot or just a lifestyle business?
How does your technology map today to where the market will be tomorrow?
How will you use this capital to reach your next valuation milestone?
what_founders_say_about_them: Founders describe them as highly efficient, noting that they 'cut the hassle and complexity' of early-stage fundraising and provide a vast network of 100+ mentors to help refine products and go-to-market strategies.
what_others_say_about_them: The press and industry recognize them as a disruptive, high-volume micro-VC that pioneered the use of founder-friendly SAFE notes in India and acts as a major feeder for top-tier VCs like Sequoia and Nexus.
red_flags_for_this_fund: Startups that project linear rather than exponential growth; founders who view their startup as a 'lifestyle business' to support their personal income; and complex cap tables that resist the simple iSAFE structure.
yt_summary: Partner Ninad Karpe emphasizes that 100X.VC is looking for startups that can grow at least 50-100% MoM; otherwise, he classifies them as 'normal' businesses rather than venture-scale opportunities. He champions the iSAFE note as the standard for removing legal friction for Day 0 founders.
